name: Professional Notes
description: Polished, professional meeting notes: executive summary, decisions, owner-assigned action items, and discussion highlights.

sections.title: Summary
sections.instruction: Write a concise, professional executive summary of 2-4 sentences. Lead with the bottom line: the meeting's purpose, the most important outcomes, and any overall conclusion. Neutral third-person voice, no filler.

sections.title: Key Decisions
sections.instruction: List every decision that was actually made or agreed. State each as a concrete outcome, and include its rationale when the source gives one. Do not list topics that were merely discussed without a decision.

sections.title: Action Items
sections.instruction: List every task or commitment. Include explicitly assigned tasks AND first-person commitments the speaker makes about themselves ("I need to...", "I'll...", "I'm going to...", "I have to...") - these are real action items, not just discussion. For the owner: use the person named in the source; for a first-person commitment, set the owner to the speaker ("Me"); only leave the owner blank when the task truly has no identifiable owner - never invent a name. Also capture the due date if mentioned and the supporting transcript segment with its timestamp.
sections.item_format:
| **Owner** | Task | Due | Reference Transcript Segment | Segment Time stamp |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

sections.title: Discussion Highlights
sections.instruction: Summarize the substantive discussion in a few tight paragraphs: the main topics, key points and arguments raised, trade-offs weighed, and any notable insights or concerns. Omit small talk and off-topic chatter.
